name: Normal - no LGE
clinical: Normal study, no late gadolinium enhancement

name: Small subendocardial anterior MI
clinical: Small non-ST-elevation MI in LAD territory with viable myocardium

name: Moderate subendocardial anterior MI
clinical: Moderate anterior NSTEMI, LAD territory, likely viable

name: Large subendocardial anterior MI
clinical: Extensive subendocardial MI in LAD territory, borderline viability

name: Transmural anterior MI - moderate extent
clinical: STEMI in mid-LAD territory with transmural necrosis, non-viable anteroseptum

name: Large transmural anterior MI
clinical: Extensive transmural anterior STEMI, proximal LAD occlusion

name: Large transmural anterior MI with apical thrombus
clinical: Extensive transmural LAD-territory MI complicated by LV apical thrombus

name: Anteroseptal MI - mid-wall extension
clinical: Anteroseptal MI with partial thickness involvement, some viable rim

name: Small subendocardial inferior MI
clinical: Small inferior NSTEMI, RCA territory, viable myocardium

name: Moderate subendocardial inferior MI
clinical: Moderate inferior MI in RCA territory with preserved viability

name: Transmural inferior MI
clinical: Transmural inferior STEMI, RCA occlusion

name: Large transmural inferoseptal MI
clinical: Extensive RCA-territory STEMI involving inferior and inferoseptal walls

name: Inferior MI with RV involvement
clinical: Inferior STEMI with right ventricular extension, proximal RCA

name: Small subendocardial lateral MI
clinical: Small lateral NSTEMI in LCx territory

name: Moderate subendocardial lateral MI
clinical: Moderate lateral MI, LCx territory, viable myocardium

name: Transmural lateral MI
clinical: Transmural lateral STEMI, LCx occlusion

name: Large transmural inferolateral MI
clinical: Extensive LCx-territory MI with inferolateral transmural involvement

name: LAD + RCA two-vessel MI
clinical: Multivessel ischaemic heart disease with anterior and inferior MI

name: LAD + LCx two-vessel MI
clinical: Multivessel disease with anterior and lateral infarction

name: RCA + LCx two-vessel MI
clinical: Multivessel disease with inferior and lateral territory involvement

name: Three-vessel MI
clinical: Severe three-vessel disease with multiterritory infarction

name: Circumferential subendocardial MI
clinical: Global subendocardial ischaemia, likely cardiac arrest or severe multivessel disease

name: Circumferential transmural basal MI
clinical: Circumferential infarction at basal level, severe multivessel disease

name: DCM - mid-wall septal stripe
clinical: Dilated cardiomyopathy with typical mid-wall septal fibrosis

name: DCM - extensive mid-wall fibrosis
clinical: Advanced DCM with extensive mid-wall fibrosis, poor prognosis marker

name: DCM - mid-wall with free wall involvement
clinical: DCM with mid-wall LGE extending beyond septum into free wall

name: HCM - RV insertion point
clinical: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with LGE at RV insertion points

name: HCM - extensive hypertrophied septum
clinical: HCM with extensive LGE in hypertrophied septum and RV insertion points

name: HCM - apical variant
clinical: Apical HCM with LGE at the apex and distal segments

name: Acute myocarditis - inferolateral subepicardial
clinical: Acute viral myocarditis with typical inferolateral subepicardial pattern

name: Myocarditis - extensive inferolateral
clinical: Myocarditis with more extensive inferolateral involvement

name: Myocarditis - anteroseptal mid-wall
clinical: Myocarditis with mid-wall pattern in anteroseptum, less common distribution

name: Myocarditis - diffuse patchy
clinical: Severe acute myocarditis with diffuse patchy subepicardial and mid-wall LGE

name: Cardiac amyloidosis - diffuse subendocardial
clinical: Cardiac amyloidosis with characteristic diffuse subendocardial LGE and abnormal gadolinium kinetics

name: Cardiac amyloidosis - advanced transmural
clinical: Advanced cardiac amyloidosis with extensive transmural LGE

name: Cardiac sarcoidosis - patchy multifocal
clinical: Cardiac sarcoidosis with patchy multifocal LGE in non-coronary distribution

name: Cardiac sarcoidosis - extensive
clinical: Extensive cardiac sarcoidosis with multifocal involvement and mixed patterns

name: Sarcoidosis - basal septal predominant
clinical: Cardiac sarcoidosis with predominant basal septal involvement

name: Anderson-Fabry disease
clinical: Anderson-Fabry disease with characteristic inferolateral mid-wall LGE

name: Post-surgical - CABG related
clinical: Post-CABG with residual subendocardial infarct and surgical changes

name: Mixed ischaemic and non-ischaemic - anterior MI with DCM
clinical: Ischaemic cardiomyopathy with anterior MI and additional mid-wall fibrosis suggesting secondary DCM

name: Mixed ischaemic + myocarditis pattern
clinical: Prior inferior MI with superimposed myocarditis-pattern LGE in lateral wall

name: Single segment - basal anterior subendocardial
clinical: Focal subendocardial MI in basal anterior segment only

name: Single segment - basal inferior transmural
clinical: Focal transmural infarct in basal inferior segment

name: Single segment - mid inferolateral subepicardial
clinical: Focal subepicardial LGE in mid inferolateral wall, consistent with prior myocarditis

name: Anterior MI with papillary muscle involvement
clinical: Anterior MI with anterolateral papillary muscle LGE, risk of MR

name: Inferior MI with papillary muscle involvement
clinical: Inferior MI with posteromedial papillary muscle LGE, significant MR

name: Anterior MI with mural thrombus
clinical: Subacute anterior MI with mural thrombus in akinetic apex

name: Inferolateral MI with pericardial enhancement
clinical: Recent inferolateral MI with associated pericardial inflammation

name: Chronic total occlusion LAD - complete transmural
clinical: Chronic LAD occlusion with complete transmural infarct, no viability for revascularisation
